other_observers: None, but the mysterious "Stan Roth" reported refinding the bird later that day on Cobirders!; I think this bird, or other Mew Gulls, have been reported (e.g., by Ted Floyd) through out the winter in eastern Boulder County.
specific_location: NW portion of Thomas Reservoir along 119th St.
description: This gull-shaped bird stood out in the front of a flock of several hundred, mainly adult Ring-billed and Herring Gulls. The bird was slightly smaller than the Ring-billed Gulls next to it. In addition it had a noticably smaller more delicate bill which appeared to be almost entirely dark.  The overall plumage of the bird was a warm, dark brown with the tail and primaries being more black than brown. The mantle still seemed more brown than dark gray, and even the head and chest remained fairly dark brown. Leg color not noted.Behaviors: The bird was standing in idly in a large flock of resting gulls.Call: silentPlumage: First winter or juvenile
